page: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for workloads requiring GPU acceleration alongside modest CPU and memory resources. It features 4 vCPUs allocated on a shared Intel Broadwell architecture running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 20.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, providing 8 GB of dedicated VRAM, and is bundled with 360 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks. For a small 135M model, it achieves 9881.81 tokens per second for prompt processing and 465.27 tokens per second for text generation. For a medium 7B model, it delivers 3161.22 tokens per second for prompt processing and 107.91 tokens per second for text generation. Conversely, CPU-bound tasks like multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database operations score in the poor and weak categories, respectively, reflecting the shared nature of the Intel Broadwell CPU cores.

This resource profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for developers needing GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated physical GPU. The combination of fractional Ampere architecture and local NVMe storage makes it highly suitable for lightweight machine learning inference, model prototyping, and low-concurrency CUDA-accelerated applications, though it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded CPU computing or high-throughput database hosting.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring 4 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, 20.0 GB of system memory, and 360 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It is equipped with a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU delivering 8 GB of VRAM. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in LLM inference, achieving 3161.22 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model. However, CPU-heavy operations like multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database tasks perform in the lower tiers due to the shared CPU allocation. This configuration represents a cost-efficient option for GPU-accelerated workloads that do not require a full dedicated GPU. It is best suited for lightweight machine learning inference, model prototyping, and small-scale CUDA-based development.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram is a fractional GPU instance with 4 shared Intel vCPUs, 20 GB RAM, 360 GB NVMe storage, and an NVIDIA A40 GPU with 8 GB VRAM, optimized for lightweight LLM inference.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-4c-20g-8vram features 4 shared vCPUs, 20 GB RAM, 360 GB NVMe, and a fractional NVIDIA A40 GPU with 8 GB VRAM for LLM inference.
tagline: A fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for cost-effective machine learning inference and prototyping.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM
4 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 20.0 GB of system memory
360 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier performance in small and medium LLM inference workloads
Cost-efficient alternative to dedicated GPU instances for prototyping
Best suited for lightweight machine learning, CUDA development, and inference
